Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to create an event log for process mining Use order_id as case ID Each event row should have Case ID Activity Name Timestamp Resource Actor optional but recommended and other attributes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First identify possible activities Order Created from Orders table timestamp order_date resource maybe sales_person_id Items Picked from Order Lines each line has item_picking_date and picker_id Order Dispatched from Delivery table each delivery has dispatch_date Order Arrived from Delivery table arrival_date Invoice Issued from Invoice table invoice_issue_date Payment Received from Payments table payment_date

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Should we create one event per item picking Probably yes because each picking is a distinct activity and may be performed by different pickers at different times

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Resource carrier_id but carrier is not a person maybe actor is carrier entity but we could use carrier_id as resource

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We may also have events like Order Completed Not explicitly but we can infer end of process maybe after payment Possibly after arrival However we might not need an explicit Complete event

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Typical order to cash process order pick ship invoice payment So we might not need an explicit Complete event

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We should also include attributes For each event we can bring in other columns that are relevant for Order Created customer_id customer_type order_priority for Item Picked item_id quantity for Delivery delivery_id carrier_id delivery_note for Invoice invoice_id invoice_amount for Payment payment_id payment_method payment_reference

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I will map to names from Resources table where available else keep ID

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Order Created timestamp order_date Activity Order Created Event Resource name of sales_person_id For Item Picked use Order Lines rows Activity Item Picked Timestamp item_picking_date Event Resource name from Resources for picker_id Also set Item ID and Quantity For Order Dispatched Activity Order Dispatched Timestamp delivery_dispatch_date Event Resource carrier name Also set Delivery ID and Delivery Note For Order Arrived Activity Order Arrived Timestamp delivery_arrival_date Event Resource same carrier For Invoice Issued Activity Invoice Issued Timestamp invoice_issue_date Event Resource billing clerk name Also set Invoice ID and Invoice Amount For Payment Received timestamp payment_date Resource System Also set Payment ID Payment Method Payment Reference and Invoice ID

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: No delivery for 1004 No invoice for 1004

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order 1004 has only Order Created and Item Picked Thats okay process mining can handle incomplete cases

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Resources mapping SP 001 Alice Brown SP 002 Bob Smith SP 003 Charlie Kim WH 001 David Lin WH 002 Eva Johnson WH 003 Frank Miller CR 01 GlobalTransport CR 02 FastShip CR 03 AirExpress BL 001 Gina Carter BL 002 Harry Lee BL 003 Irene Park

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Total events count Orders 5 Order Lines 9 Delivery records 5 gives 10 events Invoice 5 Payments 5 total 34 events

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: To keep table concise but informative I will include columns Case ID Activity Timestamp Resource Customer ID Customer Type Order Priority Sales Person Item ID Quantity Delivery ID Delivery Note Invoice ID Invoice Amount Payment ID Payment Method Payment Reference

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to join Orders data to all events to bring customer_id customer_type order_priority and sales person as case attributes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check timeline order creation picks dispatches arrivals invoices payments Good

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For Payment events Resource System because no explicit actor is given

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We assume that each item picking is an event not aggregated delivery dispatch and arrival are separate events and multiple deliveries invoices and payments belong to the same case
